title: HashiCorp Nomad Job
description: A Nomad job specification defining the workload to be scheduled, including task groups, tasks, resources, networking, service discovery, and update strategies.

ID.description: The unique identifier of the job, used to reference it in API calls.

Name.description: The human-readable name of the job.

Namespace.description: The namespace the job is registered in. Defaults to 'default'.

Type.description: The scheduler type for the job.

Priority.description: The priority of the job, from 1 (lowest) to 100 (highest). Defaults to 50.

Region.description: The region in which the job is registered.

Datacenters.description: A list of datacenters the job is allowed to run in.

NodePool.description: The node pool the job should be scheduled in.

Status.description: The current status of the job. Set by Nomad, not the user.

Stable.description: Whether this job version is marked as stable.

Version.description: The version number of the job, incremented on each update.

SubmitTime.description: The time the job was submitted in nanoseconds since Unix epoch.

Meta.description: User-defined metadata key-value pairs attached to the job.

TaskGroups.description: The task groups that make up the job. Each group is a set of co-located tasks.

Constraints.description: Job-level constraints that apply to all task groups.

Affinities.description: Job-level affinities for soft scheduling preferences.

Spreads.description: Job-level spread targets for distributing allocations.

TaskGroup.description: A task group is a set of tasks that must be co-located on the same node.

Name.description: The name of the task group.

Count.description: The number of instances of this task group to run.

Tasks.description: The tasks that are part of this task group.

Networks.description: Network configuration for the task group.

Services.description: Service registrations for the task group.

Volumes.description: Volume declarations for the task group.

Task.description: A task is the smallest unit of work in Nomad, executed within a task group.

Driver.description: The task driver to use (e.g., docker, exec, raw_exec, java, podman).

MaxFiles.description: Maximum number of rotated log files to retain.

MaxFileSizeMB.description: Maximum size of each log file in megabytes.

Templates.description: Template configurations for rendering dynamic files.

Artifacts.description: Artifacts to download before task execution.

Leader.description: If true, this task is the leader task of the group. When it exits, all other tasks are stopped.

KillTimeout.description: Time in nanoseconds to wait for the task to exit before forcefully killing it.

ShutdownDelay.description: Duration in nanoseconds to wait before sending a kill signal, allowing for graceful shutdown.

Lifecycle.description: Lifecycle configuration for sidecar and init tasks.

Hook.description: When to run the task relative to the main tasks.

Sidecar.description: If true, the task runs for the lifetime of the allocation.

Cores.description: Number of dedicated CPU cores required.

MemoryMaxMB.description: Maximum memory in megabytes for memory oversubscription.

NetworkResource.description: Network resource configuration for a task group.

Port.description: A port mapping for a network resource.

Label.description: The label for the port, used to reference it in service definitions.

Value.description: The static port value (for reserved ports).

To.description: The port inside the allocation to map to.

HostNetwork.description: The host network to bind to.

Service.description: A service registration for Consul or Nomad native service discovery.

Tags.description: Tags to associate with the service registration.

CanaryTags.description: Tags to set on the service when it is a canary.

PortLabel.description: The port label to associate with the service.

Provider.description: The service discovery provider to use.

ServiceCheck.description: A health check for a service registration.

Name.description: The name of the health check.

Path.description: The HTTP path for HTTP checks.

Protocol.description: The protocol for the check (http or https).

PortLabel.description: The port label to use for the check.

Interval.description: How often to run the check in nanoseconds.

Timeout.description: Timeout for the check in nanoseconds.

Command.description: The command to run for script checks.

MaxParallel.description: Maximum number of allocations to update simultaneously.

HealthCheck.description: The health check strategy for deployed allocations.

MinHealthyTime.description: Minimum time in nanoseconds an allocation must be healthy before considered successful.

HealthyDeadline.description: Deadline in nanoseconds for a single allocation to become healthy.

ProgressDeadline.description: Deadline in nanoseconds for overall deployment progress.

AutoRevert.description: If true, automatically reverts to the last stable version on deployment failure.

AutoPromote.description: If true, automatically promotes canary allocations when all are healthy.

Canary.description: The number of canary allocations to create during an update.

Stagger.description: Time in nanoseconds to wait between batches of updates.

Enabled.description: Whether the periodic job is enabled.

Spec.description: The cron expression for the job schedule.

ProhibitOverlap.description: If true, prevents launching new instances while previous ones are running.

TimeZone.description: The time zone for the cron expression (e.g., America/New_York).

Payload.description: Whether the payload is optional, required, or forbidden.

LTarget.description: The left-hand side target of the constraint.

RTarget.description: The right-hand side target of the constraint.

LTarget.description: The left-hand side target of the affinity.

RTarget.description: The right-hand side target of the affinity.

Weight.description: The weight of the affinity, from -100 to 100.

Spread.description: A spread target for distributing allocations across attribute values.

Weight.description: The weight of the spread, from 0 to 100.

Interval.description: The duration in nanoseconds for which the restart attempts are tracked.

Attempts.description: Maximum number of restart attempts within the interval.

Delay.description: Duration in nanoseconds to wait between restart attempts.

Mode.description: The restart behavior after exceeding attempts.

EphemeralDisk.description: Configuration for the ephemeral disk of a task group.

SizeMB.description: The size of the ephemeral disk in megabytes.

Sticky.description: If true, attempts to place the allocation on the same node as a previous allocation.

Migrate.description: If true, migrates the disk data from a previous allocation.

VolumeRequest.description: A volume request for a task group.

Source.description: The source of the volume (host volume name or CSI volume ID).

ReadOnly.description: If true, the volume is mounted as read-only.

Template.description: A template for rendering dynamic configuration files.

SourcePath.description: Path to the template source file.

DestPath.description: Path where the rendered template will be written.

ChangeMode.description: Action to take when the template changes.

ChangeSignal.description: Signal to send when ChangeMode is 'signal'.

Perms.description: File permissions for the rendered template.

Envvars.description: If true, the template is parsed as environment variables.

TaskArtifact.description: An artifact to download before task execution.

GetterSource.description: The URL to download the artifact from.

RelativeDest.description: The destination path relative to the task directory.

ScalingPolicy.description: A scaling policy for dynamically adjusting task group count.

Min.description: Minimum count for the task group.

Max.description: Maximum count for the task group.

Enabled.description: Whether the scaling policy is enabled.
